title: Invalid Input Error Type mismatch for SET OPERATION in UNION statement
description:
### What happens?

Using version 1.1.3 I execute the following statement:
```
create or replace table xx as select w from (values ('a'),('b'),('c'),('d'),('e')) t(w);

select w from (from xx limit 4) -- with 'limit 3' it works!!
CROSS JOIN (select 1 as f1) p
WHERE
     w IN (
        SELECT 'a'
        UNION -- with 'UNION ALL' it works also using 'limit 4'
        SELECT 'b'
        UNION
        SELECT 'c' WHERE p.f1 = 1
        UNION
        SELECT 'd' WHERE p.f1 = 1
);
```
This throws the following error:
```
Invalid Input Error: Type mismatch for SET OPERATION.
```
I've verified that this also occurs in v1.1.0, but it doesn't reproduce in v1.0.0.

### To Reproduce

```
create or replace table xx as select w from (values ('a'),('b'),('c'),('d'),('e')) t(w);

select w from (from xx limit 4) -- with 'limit 3' it works!!
CROSS JOIN (select 1 as f1) p
WHERE
     w IN (
        SELECT 'a'
        UNION -- with 'UNION ALL' it works also using 'limit 4'
        SELECT 'b'
        UNION
        SELECT 'c' WHERE p.f1 = 1
        UNION
        SELECT 'd' WHERE p.f1 = 1
);
```

### OS:

Windows 11 64-bit operating system

### DuckDB Version:

1.1.3

### DuckDB Client:

Cli

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Francesco Gargiulo

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, name VARCHAR(255), value FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
SELECT name FROM test_table WHERE value IN (SELECT value FROM test_table WHERE id < 3 UNION SELECT NULL AS value UNION SELECT 10.5);
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INTEGER, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO another_table SELECT id, name FROM test_table WHERE created_at > '2023-10-01 00:00:00' UNION ALL SELECT 4, 'New Entry';
